checks.spec_conformance: FINAL-DESIGN verified: 4.2 p_eff<0.50 buckets + (wins+100)/(n+200) cap 0.56; 5.2/SC3 single 7d<-2c haircut with >=-1c release hysteresis; 5.3/M2 seeds count toward guard minimums never qhat; MF5 kill mean<=-2c min 200; MF6 skip finality at window close

corrections: P5 overclaims 'can never delay a trading decision' / 'no pre-registered arm's entry timing can shift': (a) the 2.5s re-poll sleep shifts the free-running poll loop's phase (main loop is tick+sleep(TICK_S=4), not wall-clock aligned), so first-poll timing in SUBSEQUENT intervals can land up to 2.5s later - same order as existing network jitter, no decision-rule change; (b) the deferred re-poll completion performs one HTTP book fetch (timeout 11s) at end-of-tick even while an entry window is open, so a hung fetch can delay the next evaluation - within the existing multi-fetch failure envelope. Within-interval claim (defer at left>=145 < reversal2's 150s window) verified correct.
P5 claims 9 new selftests; the diff adds 12 ok() assertions (103-91=12; the 4 non-qualifying rejections are 4 checks). P6's total (41 added, 62->103) is correct.

P4's 'first-fill cap' is implemented as an every-fillable-poll ask cap (a 48c re-poll inside the window is also skipped, not only first fills). This matches FINDINGS R1's actual prescription (freeze the f>0 implicit limit order as an explicit cap) and observed refills were ~11c cheaper, so no practical divergence - naming nit only.

P2's 'n7=+1.61c on 158 matching R8-repro' matches only qualitatively (no guard fires); R8-repro's counterfactual was +1.22c on 144 (first-poll basis, smaller book). Unit's own E-section caveat (operated -2.40c still prices 9 never-entered legacy signals at rich first-poll costs) verified: those 9 run -19.64c/sh and mechanically drag the preview.

P1 edge (harmless, noting for the record): a record left final=False by a mid-window restart is not finalized until the NEXT signal appends (finalize hook matches only the current interval's t0 against ms[-1]); its skip-histogram increment is delayed and its sized stamp stays provisional; opCost basis still resolves correctly via bestCost fallback.

P3's epoch fence only protects states saved AFTER the patch (legacy states deliberately load epoch=0), so a pre-patch stale-state resurrection remains unfenced - the documented zero-live-behavior-change tradeoff, acceptable.
